title: lichen_pubkey_to_iid sets U/L bit but EUI-64 derivation flips it - inconsistent IID semantics
description:
Files: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/ipv6_addr.c:29-39,42-55

Two IID derivation functions use opposite U/L bit semantics:

lichen_eui64_to_iid (line 38): XORs U/L bit (flips it)
  iid[0] ^= UL_BIT;

lichen_pubkey_to_iid (line 53): ORs U/L bit (sets it)  
  iid[0] = (iid[0] | UL_BIT) & 0xFE;

If a pubkey's first byte already has U/L bit set, lichen_pubkey_to_iid is a no-op on that bit, but lichen_eui64_to_iid would flip it.

Risk: Contributors will assume both functions produce IIDs via the same transformation. Mixing pubkey-derived and EUI-derived IIDs in the same network could cause address conflicts if the bit patterns happen to collide after their different transformations.

Document the semantic difference in the header, or unify to a single transformation.
